currentUse: Diocese-owned secondary church of the consolidated St. Joseph the Worker Parish, Frackville (Diocese of Allentown); regular parish Masses are held at the St. Ann site (49 North Line Street), with the Annunciation BVM church building reported as irregularly used; a chapel/youth center at 27 S Broad Mountain Ave in the old Lithuanian complex is used for Eucharistic Adoration, and the former Annunciation rectory address (7 S Broad Mountain Ave) serves as the parish office.
summary: The Annunciation BVM (Šv. M. Marijos Apsireiškimo) church building still stands at its Broad Mountain Avenue site in Frackville, its 1934 cornerstone still reading "Apreiškimo Panelės Švenč. Bažnyčia," with Lithuanian stained glass, an Our Lady of Vilnius painting, and a sun-cross on the tower, though some Lithuanian details were removed after it ceased to be an official Lithuanian parish. It is no longer an independent parish: it is one of three churches (St. Ann, Annunciation BVM, St. Joseph) grouped under St. Joseph the Worker Parish in the Diocese of Allentown, whose pastor is Rev. Christopher Zelonis and whose parish office sits at 7 S Broad Mountain Ave. Regular Masses (Saturday 5:30 pm vigil, Sunday 11:00 am, plus Monday and Thursday daily Masses) are listed at the St. Ann location at 49 North Line Street, while the Annunciation building itself is described by Global True Lithuania as "irregularly used." The parish still maintains the Annunciation BVM Cemetery among its cemeteries. The adjacent Lithuanian Museum and Cultural Center, established 1982, closed in 2022 and its collection was relocated to Shenandoah, where a new Lithuanian Heritage Museum opened in September 2025 in the former Chaikowsky Funeral Home at 318 East Centre Street. The Lithuanian Days festival, which the Annunciation parish hall (built 1957) hosted in the 2010s, has moved on: the Knights of Lithuania Council 144 list the next festival for August 15-16, 2026 at Catalpa Grove in Barnesville, PA. No sale, demolition, or diocesan closure announcement for the Annunciation building was found.

developments.headline: Annunciation BVM absorbed into consolidated St. Joseph the Worker Parish; regular Masses now at St. Ann site
developments.detail: St. Joseph the Worker Parish, Frackville (Diocese of Allentown) lists three churches — St. Ann, Annunciation B.V.M., and St. Joseph — under one parish led by Rev. Christopher Zelonis, with a permanent deacon and lay staff. The parish's published Mass schedule (Saturday 5:30 pm vigil, Sunday 11:00 am, Monday 5:30 pm, Thursday 8:30 am) is listed at the St. Ann location, 49 North Line Street, with Adoration at a Chapel and Youth Center at 27 S Broad Mountain Ave; the parish office uses the former Annunciation rectory address at 7 S Broad Mountain Ave. The parish also maintains the Annunciation B.V.M. Cemetery.

developments.headline: Church building standing but 'irregularly used'; Lithuanian character partly intact
developments.detail: Global True Lithuania documents that the 1934 cornerstone inscription 'Apreiškimo Panelės Švenč. Bažnyčia 1934' remains, the interior retains Lithuanian-inscribed stained glass and an Our Lady of Vilnius painting, and the tower is crowned by a Lithuanian sun-cross, but 'the church is now irregularly used' and some Lithuanian details were removed after it ceased being an official Lithuanian parish.

sources.title: Shenandoah and southern Coal Region: Lithuanian heritage

developments.headline: Adjacent Lithuanian Museum and Cultural Center (est. 1982) closed, relocated to Shenandoah
developments.detail: Global True Lithuania states the Lithuanian Museum and Cultural Center near the church, established in 1982 with 19th-century immigrant artifacts, 'has been closed down in 2022, to be relocated to Shenandoah.'

sources.title: Shenandoah and southern Coal Region: Lithuanian heritage

developments.headline: Successor Lithuanian Heritage Museum opened in Shenandoah with the Frackville collection
developments.detail: The Shenandoah Sentinel reported the Lithuanian Heritage Museum opened at 318 East Centre Street, Shenandoah, in the former Chaikowsky Funeral Home, founded/curated by Anne Chaikowsky La Voie Skirmantas; it is described as a revival of the Knights of Lithuania museum that existed in Frackville, and 'many of the artifacts from that museum are present at the new museum.' Open by appointment.

sources.title: Shenandoah's Lithuanian heritage on display at new museum

developments.headline: Lithuanian Days festival continues, but away from the parish — next at Catalpa Grove, Barnesville, Aug 15-16, 2026
developments.detail: Knights of Lithuania Council 144 list the next annual Lithuanian Days for Saturday-Sunday August 15-16, 2026 at Catalpa Grove, 26 Park Ave, Barnesville, PA. Global True Lithuania notes the Annunciation BVM parish hall (built 1957) hosted Lithuanian Days in the 2010s but the festival has 'since moved elsewhere.'

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: Refinements to the archive record, no hard contradictions: (1) Archive status "still open / standing / survived" needs nuance — the building stands and remains diocesan property, but the Lithuanian parish no longer exists as an independent parish; Annunciation BVM is now one of three churches of the consolidated St. Joseph the Worker Parish, regular Masses are listed at the St. Ann site (49 N Line St), and Global True Lithuania describes the Annunciation church itself as "irregularly used," with some Lithuanian details removed after it ceased being an official Lithuanian parish. (2) Archive "adjacent Lithuanian museum since 1982" is outdated — the museum closed in 2022 and its collection moved to a new Lithuanian Heritage Museum in Shenandoah that opened September 2025. (3) Archive "Founded 1914" refers to the parish; the current church building's cornerstone reads 1934. (4) The Lithuanian Days festival the parish hosted (archive notes 103rd in 2017) no longer takes place at the parish hall — the 2026 festival is scheduled for Catalpa Grove, Barnesville. (5) Archive "Closed: not stated" — no formal closure decree for the building was found; the change was consolidation into a territorial parish, not closure or sale.
gaps: Could not verify the exact date or decree of the merger into St. Joseph the Worker Parish — the Diocese of Allentown's 2008 diocese-wide consolidation (151 to ~104 parishes) appeared only in search snippets (en-academic.com Wikipedia mirror), not in a page I could confirm firsthand, so no merger year is asserted in the record. Could not determine whether any Masses are still celebrated inside the Annunciation building or how often ("irregularly used" per Global True Lithuania is undated); the parish's July 2026 bulletin PDF is a scanned image that could not be machine-read to confirm per-building usage. The discovermass.com listing shows an active Saturday/Sunday schedule at the Annunciation address but appears stale (carries an old COVID-19 notice) and was not relied on. No real-estate listing, sale price, buyer, demolition permit, or diocesan closure/relocation announcement for the Annunciation church, hall, or former museum building was found in searches. The reason the museum left Frackville (and whether its former building on the parish campus is now vacant or sold) is not stated in any opened source.
